title: Level 660
wikitext:

LEVEL 660
"MANDRAKE MORTALITY"
{{RecommendedAudioNest|audio={{Audio|round=yes|file=Lethal Company - Eclipsed Theme.mp3|align=center|loop=true|volume=0.4}}|text=enable page ambience for best experience:}}



----
{{Blurquote|image=Corruptionterraria2.jpg|quote-text=
You open your eyes to the sight of a dilapidated, otherworldly field - a sickly purple mist enveloping the landscape you could barely call "natural" anymore. Dread floods your mind, though with a twisted sense of curiosity, you begin creeping down the road. Each step breaks the eerie stillness of your surroundings. You stand out from this landscape, like a festering tumor on a perfect face. Still, you continue.

The grass seems to draw you in further with its delightful aura. ''How could I resist?'' You desired anything more than the dreadful, solid concrete stretching to infinity. You craved the gentle embrace of the soft grass.

So you lay on the grass. It embraces you. A flurry of spores flood your surroundings, making each breath a mind-breaking agony. Your vision decays, and you are consumed into the perfection of Mandrake Mortality.

}}
----


{{Foreground|File=Ourple fog.gif|Opacity=0.3}} {{Class 5|hexagon=4|color-h=280|color-s=100%|color-l=24%|class=4|safety=Unsafe|security=Fluctuating Stability|entity=Non-Sentient Biological Hazards}}
OVERVIEW
---- [[File:Terrariacorruptionlevel.jpg|400x400px|right|link=]] [[File:Corruptionterraria2.jpg|400x400px|right|link=]] [[File:Corruptionterraria3.jpg|400x800px|right|link=]] '''Level 660,''' otherwise known as '''"Mandrake Mortality",''' is an expanse of rural, dilapidated roads drowned in a violet hue. The surrounding fields are scattered with a surplus of vegetation including trees, grass, bushes, etc. on the brink of wilting, merely supported by an unintelligibly "dead" force. Wind, along with all other natural motion (save for gravity's influence), is entirely absent in '''Level 660,''' leaving an eerie stillness hanging in the musty air. The electrical drone of power lines serves as the only native sound heard through the level; despite this, no current or charge has been detected in these lines. '''Level 660''' rarely shifts in weather conditions, remaining at a constant overcast and a temperature of roughly 12°C (53.6°F). Rain, albeit scarcely documented, often comes in high amounts, though will instantly evaporate upon making contact with the ground. In the favor of plant life, however; the ground is naturally rich in water and minerals. Despite the thriving condition of the plants, the soil is exceptionally acidic with a pH level of ~3.5. As a result, these plants naturally consume and exhibit an abundance of iron. The oxygenated iron - in tandem with water vapor - supplies a faint blood-like scent to the level's air. '''Level 660's''' manmade structures primarily consist of electrical equipment, such as rusted pylons and rotten poles. All attempts at tracing down the source of these lines have proven fruitless, as the level possesses a nearly unnoticeable geometrical anomaly that copies the environment as one enters regions that have been unexplored. Copied terrain will display an exact replica of the furthest unique patch of land from itself, including the structures in the section such as poles and pylons. Recently copied terrain is unaffected by the purple shade and thus uninfected by the parasitic mist, displaying fresh and healthy plant life. In a short matter of time, the generated landscapes are swiftly consumed by the mist and reanimated into undead plants. The mist ravaging the air of '''Level 660''' is informally classified as '''Corrupt Impurity,''' a level-based object in the database. It is a hazy smog of a translucent mulberry shade, with '''Level 660''' being its origin. '''Corrupt Impurity''' is able to sporadically replicate itself in irregular patterns, making it infeasible to practically store due to its constant pressure increase in a container. '''Corrupt Impurity's''' most notable property is its parasitic behavior towards plants; when in contact with a cell wall, the object will spread throughout the plant and gradually decay its internal structure. Consequently, the plant gains a deep violet color and appears to be possessed by an "undead force". Upon complete infection, the host will adopt one of seven traits based on the seven deadly sins:
{| class="fandom-table" |+Infected Plant Variants !Name !Frequency !Properties |- |Envy | 0.08 |Clones the properties of its neighboring infected plants. Roughly half as effective as the neigbor plant, though can clone up to 4 properties at once. |- |Gluttony |0.27 |Able to use nearly all matter to develop biological mass and gain energy, regardless of nutrient value. Serves as food to the native life of the level, distinguished by a blue hue. |- |Greed | 0.03 |Absorbs as much nutrients as possible within its environment to grow at a rapid pace. This continues until nutrients run short or the plant collapses. |- |Lust |0.16 | Passively produces excessive amounts of pollen. When inhaled into the lungs, pollen from a lust-oriented plant may cause severe inflammation and swelling. |- |Pride |0.33 |Roots spray an extremely acidic vapor at all nearby life, melting their biological matter into a nutritious goo. This goo provides vibrant color to its leaves when consumed by the plant. |- |Sloth |0.05 | Latches onto nearby biological matter, gradually harvesting the nutrients from its source. Animals are primarily infected via the eyes or inner mouth. |- |Wrath |0.08 | Produces growths of stringy, waving thorns which can inject a potent neurotoxin. This venom is able to shut down brain activity within 2 hours in exceptionally high doses. |}
Because '''Corrupt Impurity''' spreads through cell walls, non-plant life is unable to be directly infected by the object. The listed properties that plants can attain through infection only include the documented effects in '''Level 660;''' other levels with plants infected by '''Corrupt Impurity''' may exhibit different properties depending on the conditions of the individual level's environment, and infected plants in '''Level 660''' may have properties based on other sins. These observations have lead to the theory that '''Level 660''' possesses an undetectable force that shifts based on all "sinful" actions committed by those in the Frontrooms - altering the effect of '''Corrupt Impurity''' on plants depending on variety and severity. An estimated 23% of plants in '''Level 660''' exhibit sins outside of the recognized seven. {{Blurimage|file=Purpleguybug.webp|align=center|width=550px|caption=Closeup of an unidentified beetle species after long-term exposure to corrupt plant pollen. Note the white eyes and contracted limbs.}}
ECOSYSTEM
---- '''Level 660's''' only native life (excluding plants) consists of small mammals and insects originating from the Frontrooms. Hence the level's namesake, the mortality rate of animals to the level's environment is exceptionally high, with approximately 90% of total deaths being caused by infected plants. The surplus of hazardous plant life results in a high-stress ecosystem for all diets alike. The only reliable method of feeding for life in '''Level 660''' is of gluttony-oriented plants; they are rather resistant to the toxic chemicals produced by various infected plant forms and tend to grow in groups. Gluttony-oriented plants are distinguished by their subtle blue color in contrast to the other violet foliage, consequently filtering out many animals with poor vision. No carnivorous mammals are known to exist in '''Level 660.'''
ENTRANCES & EXITS
---- ENTRANCES *Falling into a purple patch of quicksand in [[Level 427]] will lead here. *Entering a purple door in [[Level 754]] will lead here. *[[Level 630]], [[Level 666|Level 666]], and [[Level 66]] have an exceptionally low chance to suddenly transition into '''Level 660.''' EXITS * Certain areas may cause one to feel the air becoming heavier. If one touches a pylon with "5162020" etched onto it in these areas, they will be sent to [[Level 410]] or [[Level 60|Level Sixty.]] *This level possesses a notably higher chance to cause visions which may transition into [[The Grand Medium|the Grand Medium.]] *Stepping into a puddle may cause the level to transition into [[Level 71|Level 71.]] *Rarely, when consuming '''Wrath''', you may faint and wake up at [[Level 626.2]]. *Whether eclipses can occur in '''Level 660''' is unknown, though it is rumored that experiencing the event will send one to [[Level 700|Level 700.]] {{Redirect|Prev=659|Next=661}} {{Collapse|num=1|initial=> Open Contributors & Media Sources|final=> Close Contributors & Media Sources|body= Author: [[User:Lemonmuffinn|Lemon]]
Inspired by Terraria's corruption biome by Re-Logic and The Colour Out of Space by H.P. Lovecraft
